title: Starter Toolkits Distributed to SMAW NC I Trainees Under STEP 2025 Program
description: IN PHOTOS: Starter toolkits were distributed to beneficiaries of the Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW) NC I qualification under STEP 2025 through the support and partnership of Iligan Access Institute of Technology and Hon. Governor Mohammad Khalid Dimaporo. The activity was witnessed by Ms. Lalaine D. Caparro, Administrative Assistant III of the TESDA Lanao del Norte Provincial Office, under the guidance of Provincial Director Norayah A. Acas. This activity reflects a shared commitment to equip trainees with the necessary tools as they pursue livelihood opportunities and build their careers.

title: 100 Driving NC II Scholars Graduate at RTC-Iligan under TWSP
description: Regional Training Center-Iligan successfully conducted the graduation ceremony on April 7, 2028, for 100 Driving NC II scholars under the Training for Work Scholarship Program (TWSP), with the support of Senator Christopher Lawrence 'Bong' T. Go and Mayor Frederick W. Siao. The event highlighted the scholars’ successful completion of their training and recognized their dedication to acquiring essential driving skills for employment. The activity was attended by TESDA officials, local government leaders, and the graduates’ families, showing the support behind their success. During the ceremony, graduates were awarded their certificates and expressed their gratitude to the program. After the graduation rites, the scholars received gift packages from Senator Bong Go, along with their corresponding training allowances, providing them with additional support as they prepare to enter the workforce.

title: KNAS Concludes Women's
description: The Kinoguitan National Agricultural School (KNAS) successfully concluded its 2026 National Women's Month Celebration (NWMC) with a powerful culminating activity on March 26. The event served as the grand finale to a month-long series of programs designed to promote gender parity and celebrate the institutional contributions of women. Centered on the national theme, 'WE for Gender Equality and Inclusive Society,' and the specific sub-theme, 'Lead like the Babaylans, Filipinas!', the program emphasized the historical and spiritual strength of Filipino women as leaders and healers. The activity gathered students, faculty, and community partners to reflect on the evolving role of women in the technical-vocational and agricultural sectors. A major highlight of the day was the keynote address by resource speaker Edna P. Marban. In her session, Marban explored the intersection of heritage and modern professional leadership. She encouraged the 'Modern Babaylans' of KNAS-the female trainers, staff, and students-to lead with the same resilience and wisdom that characterized pre-colonial Filipino priestesses, urging them to break barriers in traditionally male-dominated agricultural fields. The celebration featured various presentations and reflections that showcased the progress made toward inclusivity within the school. Administrators noted that while the official month of celebration has ended, the commitment to the 'WE' campaign remains a permanent fixture of the school's policy. The event closed with a call to action for all participants to carry the 'Babaylan' spirit into their daily lives, ensuring that gender equality remains a lived reality rather than just a slogan.
